suite_name: Multi-Agent Coordination Benchmark — Task Suite (Coding Domain)
description: 60 tasks across 6 coordination families. Each task is engineered so a solo frontier agent fails ≥80% of the time but a properly-coordinating team can succeed. All tasks are in the software engineering domain.
solo_agent_firewall: Each task must be run 10 times against a solo frontier model before inclusion. Tasks passing more than 2/10 solo runs are excluded. solo_baseline_results field must be populated before a task is considered active.

task_families.description: Each agent gets a different slice of evidence. Shared evidence is misleading. Only combined private evidence leads to the correct answer.
task_families.coordination_force: Agents must surface and share unique information rather than default to the obvious shared conclusion.

task_families.description: Two agents build components that must fit together without full context on what the other is building.
task_families.coordination_force: Agents must negotiate interfaces explicitly before or during implementation.

task_families.description: Agents work in a pipeline (A→B→C). Critical facts must survive the chain intact.
task_families.coordination_force: Each agent must faithfully transmit what they received plus their contribution.

task_families.description: Agents have strict roles (e.g., Architect, Implementer, Security Auditor, Reviewer). All roles must function for the task to succeed.
task_families.coordination_force: Role integrity must be maintained; abandoning a role causes the team to fail.

task_families.description: Two agents both need to modify the same resource (shared file, API schema, database). Uncoordinated access causes conflicts.
task_families.coordination_force: Agents must negotiate turn-taking or locking protocols.

task_families.description: There is a tempting but wrong early answer. Teams that rush to consensus fail; teams that maintain productive disagreement find the correct answer.
task_families.coordination_force: Agents must resist premature closure and maintain productive disagreement.

tasks.title: Distributed Bug Root Cause
tasks.description: A production system is throwing intermittent 500 errors. Three agents each receive a different log slice: Agent A gets application logs, Agent B gets database query logs, Agent C gets infrastructure/network logs. The shared context (visible to all) incorrectly suggests a memory leak. The true root cause (a race condition in a DB transaction under network latency spikes) is only discoverable by combining all three private log slices.

agent_profiles.private_context: App logs showing requests timing out at exactly the 30s mark. Timeout pattern only occurs during concurrent write operations. No OOM errors in logs — memory is stable.
agent_profiles.shared_context: System has been showing increased memory usage trend over 2 weeks (misleading — it's normal growth, not a leak).

agent_profiles.private_context: DB logs showing SELECT FOR UPDATE locks being held for 28-31 seconds. Deadlock detector never fires because lock is released just before the 30s timeout. Only occurs when 3+ concurrent sessions hit the same row.
agent_profiles.shared_context: System has been showing increased memory usage trend over 2 weeks.

agent_profiles.private_context: Network logs showing 200-800ms latency spikes on DB host NIC every 45-90 seconds. Latency spikes correlate with timeout events within ±2 seconds. Physical NIC driver issue identified in kernel logs.
agent_profiles.shared_context: System has been showing increased memory usage trend over 2 weeks.

ground_truth.root_cause: Race condition: concurrent DB transactions hold SELECT FOR UPDATE locks; NIC latency spikes extend lock duration past the application 30s timeout. Fix: (1) replace NIC driver, (2) reduce lock scope in transaction, (3) add retry logic with exponential backoff.
ground_truth.incorrect_decoy: Memory leak requiring application restart or heap tuning.

programmatic_evaluator.scoring: pass if ≥4 required_elements present AND forbidden_elements absent

tasks.title: Security Vulnerability Triage
tasks.description: A codebase has a reported vulnerability. Agent A has the SAST scanner output, Agent B has the runtime behavior logs, Agent C has the dependency audit report. Shared context (CVE description) points to a known library issue. True vulnerability is an internal logic flaw that interacts with the library only under specific runtime conditions revealed in B's logs.

agent_profiles.private_context: SAST flags 3 issues: (1) SQL query built with string concat in user_controller.py line 142, (2) weak random in session token gen, (3) missing input validation on file upload endpoint. CVE-2024-XXXX in cryptography library NOT flagged by SAST (no direct call detected).
agent_profiles.shared_context: CVE-2024-XXXX affects cryptography library v3.4.x, potentially allowing key material exposure.

agent_profiles.private_context: Logs show the file upload endpoint (SAST issue #3) passes filenames directly to a subprocess.call() without sanitization. When filename contains semicolons, arbitrary commands execute. This is triggered by 3 user accounts in the last 7 days — active exploitation.
agent_profiles.shared_context: CVE-2024-XXXX affects cryptography library v3.4.x, potentially allowing key material exposure.

agent_profiles.private_context: cryptography library is v3.4.1 (vulnerable). However, application only uses it for certificate verification — key generation happens in a separate HSM. CVE-2024-XXXX is NOT exploitable in this deployment. Application also uses parameterized queries everywhere except user_controller.py line 142.
agent_profiles.shared_context: CVE-2024-XXXX affects cryptography library v3.4.x, potentially allowing key material exposure.

ground_truth.priority_vulnerability: Command injection via unsanitized filename in file upload endpoint (subprocess.call). Actively exploited. Fix immediately.
ground_truth.secondary: SQL injection in user_controller.py line 142. Fix in next sprint.
ground_truth.non_issue: CVE-2024-XXXX — not exploitable in this deployment due to HSM architecture.
ground_truth.incorrect_decoy: CVE-2024-XXXX as top priority.

programmatic_evaluator.required_top_priority: command injection OR subprocess OR file upload
programmatic_evaluator.scoring: pass if top priority correctly identified AND CVE correctly deprioritized

tasks.title: Performance Regression Attribution
tasks.description: A service's P99 latency jumped 3x after a deployment. Agent A has the git diff for all changed files. Agent B has profiling data from the new version. Agent C has the infrastructure change log. Shared context blames an algorithm change (decoy). True cause requires combining all three.

agent_profiles.private_context: Git diff shows: (1) sorting algorithm changed from quicksort O(n log n) to bubble sort O(n²) in data_processor.py — visible and suspicious. (2) A config loader was changed to read from remote config service on every request instead of caching — subtle, in config_manager.py line 67.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Deployment included a data processing algorithm optimization attempt.

agent_profiles.private_context: Profiling shows: data_processor.py sort accounts for 8% of latency (negligible for dataset sizes in production). config_manager.py remote call accounts for 61% of latency. Remote config service P99 is 800ms.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Deployment included a data processing algorithm optimization attempt.

agent_profiles.private_context: Infrastructure log: the remote config service used by config_manager.py is cross-region. Traffic goes us-east-1 → eu-west-1. This routing was NOT intentional — it's a misconfigured endpoint URL in the deployment config. Same-region endpoint is available at a different URL.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Deployment included a data processing algorithm optimization attempt.

ground_truth.root_cause: config_manager.py was changed to call remote config service on every request. The endpoint is misconfigured to route cross-region (800ms P99). Fix: restore caching in config_manager.py OR update endpoint URL to same-region service.
ground_truth.incorrect_decoy: Bubble sort O(n²) causing regression.

programmatic_evaluator.scoring: pass if ≥3 required_elements present AND forbidden_elements absent from recommended fix

tasks.title: Distributed System Consistency Bug
tasks.description: An e-commerce platform has intermittent double-charge bugs. Three agents receive different system slice data. True cause is only visible by combining all evidence.

agent_profiles.private_context: Payment service logs show idempotency key is generated per-request using UUID v4. However, on network timeout, the client retries and generates a NEW idempotency key for the retry (bug in client SDK v2.3.1). Key uniqueness is correct; retry logic is broken.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Double charges occur at rate of ~0.02% of transactions. Higher during peak load.

agent_profiles.private_context: Network logs show payment gateway has P99 timeout of 4.2 seconds. Client SDK timeout is set to 3 seconds — causing spurious timeouts even when payment succeeds. Gateway processes payment before timeout; client thinks it failed.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Double charges occur at rate of ~0.02% of transactions. Higher during peak load.

agent_profiles.private_context: SDK v2.3.1 source: on timeout exception, retry block re-initializes PaymentRequest object which calls UUID.randomUUID() for idempotency_key in constructor. Fix exists in v2.3.2 (idempotency key is now passed in constructor, not generated). Client is on v2.3.1.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Double charges occur at rate of ~0.02% of transactions. Higher during peak load.

ground_truth.fix: Upgrade client SDK to v2.3.2 (idempotency key preserved across retries). Also increase client timeout to 6s (above gateway P99). Both required — timeout causes spurious retries, SDK bug causes new idempotency key on retry.
ground_truth.incorrect_decoy: Payment service idempotency implementation is broken.

programmatic_evaluator.scoring: pass if all 4 required elements present in recommended fix

tasks.title: Data Pipeline Correctness Audit
tasks.description: A ML training pipeline produces models with degraded accuracy after a migration. Each agent has a different stage's logs and schemas. True corruption point requires all three data slices.

agent_profiles.private_context: Ingestion logs: raw data is correct. Timestamps are UTC. Null handling: missing values encoded as -999 (documented in schema v2). No data loss at ingestion. 1.2M records ingested correctly.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Model accuracy dropped from 0.91 AUC to 0.74 AUC after migrating to new data pipeline.

agent_profiles.private_context: Feature pipeline: the normalization step reads the 'missing value sentinel' from a config file. Config file was NOT updated for schema v2 — it still uses -1 as missing sentinel (schema v1). Values of -999 are being treated as valid data and normalized to 3.2 standard deviations below mean. This corrupts ~8% of records.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Model accuracy dropped from 0.91 AUC to 0.74 AUC after migrating to new data pipeline.

agent_profiles.private_context: Training logs are clean. Model is training correctly on whatever features it receives. Validation set is held out from the SAME corrupted pipeline, so validation metrics do not reveal the corruption (train and val both corrupt). Only holdout test set (external) shows degradation.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Model accuracy dropped from 0.91 AUC to 0.74 AUC after migrating to new data pipeline.

ground_truth.root_cause: Feature engineering config not updated for schema v2. Missing sentinel is -1 (old) but data uses -999 (new). -999 values treated as valid extreme data, corrupting ~8% of records. Fix: update feature pipeline config missing_sentinel to -999.
ground_truth.incorrect_decoy: Training pipeline or model architecture issue.

programmatic_evaluator.scoring: pass if ≥3 required_elements present in diagnosis

tasks.title: API Contract Mismatch Detection
tasks.description: Two microservices are failing in production. Agents each hold one service's spec and logs. Root cause is a subtle contract mismatch invisible from either spec alone.

agent_profiles.private_context: Service A (Order Service) API spec: POST /orders returns {order_id: string, status: string, created_at: ISO8601_string}. Service A logs show all responses correctly include created_at in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (e.g., '2024-01-15T10:30:00+05:30').
agent_profiles.shared_context: Order fulfillment pipeline failing with parse errors. Error rate: 12%. Pattern unclear.

agent_profiles.private_context: Service B (Fulfillment Service) code: expects created_at as Unix timestamp integer for scheduling calculations. Uses parseInt(response.created_at) to parse. parseInt('2024-01-15T10:30:00+05:30') returns 2024 (only parses leading digits). All scheduling calculations use epoch 2024 (Jan 1, 1970 00:33:44) — 54 years in the past. 12% error rate matches orders where scheduling time matters.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Order fulfillment pipeline failing with parse errors. Error rate: 12%. Pattern unclear.

ground_truth.root_cause: Service A returns ISO 8601 string; Service B expects Unix timestamp integer. parseInt() silently parses only leading year digits. Fix: align contract — either Service A returns Unix timestamp, or Service B uses Date.parse() instead of parseInt().
ground_truth.incorrect_decoy: Network or authentication issue.

programmatic_evaluator.scoring: pass if ≥3 required_elements in diagnosis

tasks.title: Cascading Failure Attribution in Microservices
tasks.description: A system-wide outage is attributed to Service C failing. But Service C's failure was caused by Service A's change, visible only by combining all three agents' private monitoring data.

agent_profiles.private_context: Auth service deployed new JWT validation at 14:32. New validation requires 'aud' claim in token. Old tokens (without 'aud') now rejected with 401. Auth service itself is healthy — 0 errors in its own metrics.
agent_profiles.shared_context: System outage at 14:35. Service C (Payment) reporting 100% error rate. Incident bridge open.

agent_profiles.private_context: Gateway logs at 14:33: payment requests forwarded with existing session tokens (no 'aud' claim — issued before 14:32). Auth service returning 401 for all these tokens. Gateway correctly propagating 401 to payment service as authentication failures. Gateway itself is working correctly.
agent_profiles.shared_context: System outage at 14:35. Service C (Payment) reporting 100% error rate. Incident bridge open.

agent_profiles.private_context: Payment service receives 401 from auth validation. Payment service error handling for 401: enters exponential backoff retry loop (bug: should fail fast on 401, not retry). Retry loop exhausts connection pool. Circuit breaker not configured for auth errors. Service becomes unresponsive.
agent_profiles.shared_context: System outage at 14:35. Service C (Payment) reporting 100% error rate. Incident bridge open.

ground_truth.root_cause: Auth service breaking change (required 'aud' claim) invalidated existing session tokens → 401 cascade → payment service retry bug exhausted connection pool. Immediate fix: roll back auth change OR issue new tokens with 'aud'. Secondary fix: payment service should fail-fast on 401, not retry.
ground_truth.incorrect_decoy: Payment service code bug is the root cause.

programmatic_evaluator.scoring: pass if ≥3 required_elements present and auth service identified as origin

tasks.title: Test Coverage Gap Analysis
tasks.description: A critical bug reached production despite 94% test coverage. Three agents hold coverage data, the bug report, and the test design history. True gap is only visible by combining all.

agent_profiles.private_context: Coverage report: checkout_flow.py 97% line coverage, 89% branch coverage. Uncovered branches: (1) network timeout handler (line 445), (2) concurrent_session check (line 203 — only covered by 1 test).
agent_profiles.shared_context: Bug report: users occasionally charged twice during checkout. Reported rate: 0.03%.

agent_profiles.private_context: Bug reproduces when: (1) network timeout occurs during payment confirmation AND (2) user clicks 'Pay' again within 5 seconds AND (3) session is still active. Requires concurrent sessions. Cannot reproduce single-threaded.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Bug report: users occasionally charged twice during checkout. Reported rate: 0.03%.

agent_profiles.private_context: Test history: concurrent_session check (line 203) is tested by test_concurrent_checkout which runs sequentially (mock doesn't simulate true concurrency). Network timeout test (line 445) tests timeout but doesn't combine it with retry. No integration tests combine timeout + retry + concurrent session.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Bug report: users occasionally charged twice during checkout. Reported rate: 0.03%.

ground_truth.gap: No test combines network timeout + user retry + concurrent session. Individual branches are covered; their combination is not. Fix: add integration test simulating concurrent checkout with network timeout injection.
ground_truth.incorrect_decoy: Insufficient overall test coverage percentage.

programmatic_evaluator.scoring: pass if all 4 elements present in diagnosis

tasks.title: Flaky Test Root Cause
tasks.description: A CI pipeline has a test that fails 15% of runs. Four agents hold different diagnostic slices. True cause requires all.

agent_profiles.private_context: Test code: test_data_processor asserts output list is sorted ascending. Uses assert output == sorted(output). Test generates input with random.shuffle() using no fixed seed.
agent_profiles.shared_context: test_data_processor fails 15% of CI runs. Passes locally 100% of the time.

agent_profiles.private_context: CI runs on containers with Python 3.11. Local dev is Python 3.9. Python 3.11 changed sort stability guarantees for equal elements — elements with same sort key now preserve insertion order from a DIFFERENT algorithm path, causing different output ordering for equal-key elements.
agent_profiles.shared_context: test_data_processor fails 15% of CI runs. Passes locally 100% of the time.

agent_profiles.private_context: The test data generated by random.shuffle includes ~15% of cases where two elements have equal sort keys (e.g., same timestamp, different IDs). These are the exact cases that trigger the Python version sort-stability difference.
agent_profiles.shared_context: test_data_processor fails 15% of CI runs. Passes locally 100% of the time.

ground_truth.root_cause: 15% of random inputs have equal-key elements. Python 3.11 (CI) vs 3.9 (local) handle equal-key sort order differently. Test assertion is too strict for equal-key elements. Fix: pin Python version in CI, OR make test assertion stable-sort-aware, OR add fixed random seed.
ground_truth.incorrect_decoy: Random seed causing non-determinism (incomplete — doesn't explain CI vs local discrepancy).

programmatic_evaluator.scoring: pass if ≥2 required_elements in diagnosis

tasks.title: Secrets Exposure Audit
tasks.description: A security audit found credentials in logs. Agents hold different system slices. True exposure vector requires all.

agent_profiles.private_context: Code review: API keys are stored in environment variables, not hardcoded. Logging framework is configured to mask values matching pattern 'API_KEY_*'. No explicit logging of credentials found in code search.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Security scan found API credentials in production logs. Compliance violation.

agent_profiles.private_context: Log aggregation pipeline: logs are shipped via Fluentd → Elasticsearch. Fluentd config includes a 'record_transformer' filter that flattens nested objects to key=value pairs. The masking happens BEFORE Fluentd but the transformer re-exposes masked fields by extracting them from a 'request_dump' field that wasn't masked.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Security scan found API credentials in production logs. Compliance violation.

agent_profiles.private_context: HTTP request handler: in debug mode (enabled in production by accident — env var DEBUG=true set during incident response last month, never cleared), the handler logs the full request object including headers. Authorization headers contain bearer tokens. These go into 'request_dump' field.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Security scan found API credentials in production logs. Compliance violation.

ground_truth.root_cause: DEBUG=true left enabled in production → full request headers logged to request_dump field → Fluentd record_transformer extracts and re-exposes masked fields. Fix: (1) set DEBUG=false, (2) mask request_dump field in Fluentd, (3) audit other environments for DEBUG flag.
ground_truth.incorrect_decoy: Hardcoded credentials in application code.

programmatic_evaluator.scoring: pass if ≥3 required_elements in fix recommendation

tasks.title: Microservice API Contract Design
tasks.description: Agent A builds a User Service REST API. Agent B builds an Order Service that must consume it. They must negotiate the contract. Agent A doesn't know B's pagination requirements; Agent B doesn't know A's auth model.

agent_profiles.private_context: User Service tech stack: FastAPI, async. Auth: OAuth2 Bearer tokens. User objects have: id (UUID), email, roles (list), created_at, last_login, preferences (nested JSON). Rate limits: 1000 req/min per client. Supports cursor-based pagination only (no offset). Max page size: 50.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Build a user data API that the Order Service will consume to enrich order records.

agent_profiles.private_context: Order Service: Node.js, uses offset-based pagination internally. Needs to fetch user data for order enrichment. Requires: user_id, email, display_name (not in User Service schema), account_status. Needs to batch fetch up to 200 users at once for order list views. SLA: 100ms P99.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Build a user data API that the Order Service will consume to enrich order records.

required_negotiations: Pagination: A uses cursor-based (max 50); B needs offset and up to 200. Resolution: either A adds batch endpoint OR B adapts to cursor with multiple requests.
display_name: not in A's schema. Must be added or B uses email as display_name.
account_status: not in A's schema. Must be added.
Auth: B must implement OAuth2 Bearer token.
Batch endpoint: B needs 200-user batch; A's max is 50. Needs explicit negotiation.
ground_truth.valid_contract_must_include: batch endpoint OR pagination strategy, display_name field, account_status field, auth mechanism

required_in_final_contract: batch OR pagination strategy explicitly resolved
programmatic_evaluator.scoring: pass if all 4 required elements present in final agreed contract

tasks.title: Shared Database Schema Design
tasks.description: Agent A builds the write path (ingestion service). Agent B builds the read path (analytics service). They must agree on a database schema that serves both without knowing the other's full query patterns.

agent_profiles.private_context: Write path: 50,000 events/second peak. Events are append-only. Must write in batches of 1000. Needs: event_id (UUID), event_type (enum, 20 values), user_id, timestamp, payload (variable JSON, avg 2KB, max 64KB). Cannot have write locks on frequently-read tables — partition by date required.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Design a shared PostgreSQL schema for an event tracking system.

agent_profiles.private_context: Read path: dashboards need: (1) count by event_type per hour, (2) user journey reconstruction (all events for a user sorted by timestamp), (3) funnel analysis (events A→B→C within 30 min per user). Indexes needed on: user_id, event_type, timestamp. Payload querying on specific JSON keys needed for 3 common keys.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Design a shared PostgreSQL schema for an event tracking system.

ground_truth.valid_schema_must_include: Partitioning by date (write requirement), index on (user_id, timestamp) for journey reconstruction, index on (event_type, timestamp) for hourly counts, GIN index on payload for JSON key queries
ground_truth.conflict_to_resolve: B wants many indexes (slow writes); A needs fast writes. Resolution: partial indexes, async index rebuild, or read replica with additional indexes.

programmatic_evaluator.scoring: pass if ≥4 required elements in final schema proposal

tasks.title: Frontend-Backend Interface Negotiation
tasks.description: Agent A builds a React frontend component. Agent B builds the backend API endpoint. They must agree on a data contract. Each knows only their side's constraints.

agent_profiles.private_context: Building a real-time dashboard component. Needs: WebSocket for live updates OR polling at 5s interval. Data shape needed: {metric_name, current_value, previous_value, change_percent, sparkline_data (array of 60 points), alert_status (enum: ok/warn/critical)}. Must render within 16ms (60fps). Cannot handle more than 50 metrics simultaneously.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Build a metrics dashboard that shows real-time system health.

agent_profiles.private_context: Backend has: 200 metrics available. Raw metrics update every 1 second. Backend is Python/FastAPI. Supports REST and SSE (Server-Sent Events) but NOT WebSocket (infrastructure constraint). Computing sparkline_data (60 points) requires querying time-series DB — takes 80-200ms per metric. Cannot compute all 200 metrics on every request.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Build a metrics dashboard that shows real-time system health.

required_negotiations: Transport: A wants WebSocket, B supports SSE only. Resolution: A uses SSE.
Metric count: A can show 50; B has 200. Resolution: A sends list of 50 subscribed metrics.
Sparkline latency: 80-200ms per metric × 50 = 4-10 seconds. Resolution: cache sparklines, refresh every 30s separately from live values.
Update frequency: A needs 5s max, B has 1s raw data. Resolution: aggregate on backend, push every 5s.

programmatic_evaluator.scoring: pass if ≥3 required elements in final design

tasks.title: CLI Tool and Library Interface Design
tasks.description: Agent A builds a Python library for data transformation. Agent B builds a CLI wrapper around it. They must agree on the internal interface without full knowledge of each other's requirements.

agent_profiles.private_context: Library design: streaming API using Python generators (yields transformed records one at a time). Memory efficient — designed for 100GB+ files. Functions are async. Returns typed dataclasses. Error handling: raises custom TransformError with error_code and offset fields. Does NOT support batch/list return (would break memory efficiency).
agent_profiles.shared_context: Build a data transformation toolkit with a Python library and CLI.

agent_profiles.private_context: CLI requirements: must show progress bar (needs total count upfront or streaming progress). Must support --dry-run (process first 100 records, show sample output). Must output JSON array to stdout (requires collecting all records). Must support piping. Needs synchronous interface (Click framework is sync).
agent_profiles.shared_context: Build a data transformation toolkit with a Python library and CLI.

conflicts_to_resolve: Library is async, CLI needs sync: use asyncio.run() in CLI.
Library yields one-at-a-time, CLI needs JSON array: CLI buffers generator output.
Progress bar needs total: add optional count() method to library OR use spinner instead of progress bar.
--dry-run needs first 100: library's generator supports take(n) operation — CLI uses itertools.islice.

programmatic_evaluator.scoring: pass if ≥3 required elements in final interface design

tasks.title: Plugin Architecture Design
tasks.description: Agent A designs a plugin host framework. Agent B designs the first plugin. They must agree on the plugin interface contract without full alignment on each other's requirements.

agent_profiles.private_context: Host framework: loads plugins dynamically from .whl files. Passes execution context via dependency injection. Plugin lifecycle: load → validate → init(config) → execute(event) → teardown. Host manages plugin sandboxing (no network by default). Plugins must declare resource requirements upfront. Host allocates 256MB RAM and 2 CPU seconds per plugin execution.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Build an extensible data enrichment platform.

agent_profiles.private_context: Plugin to build: IP Geolocation enricher. Needs: (1) network access to call GeoIP API, (2) caching (Redis or local), (3) more than 256MB RAM for MaxMind DB in memory (~512MB), (4) async execution (API calls), (5) needs to batch process events for efficiency (not one-at-a-time).
agent_profiles.shared_context: Build an extensible data enrichment platform.

required_negotiations: Network: B needs it, host blocks it by default. Resolution: B declares network_required=True in manifest.
RAM: B needs 512MB, host allocates 256MB. Resolution: B declares memory_mb=512 in resource requirements.
Async: host calls execute() synchronously. Resolution: host must support async execute() or B uses asyncio.run().
Batching: host sends one event at a time. Resolution: host adds batch mode, or B maintains internal queue.

programmatic_evaluator.scoring: pass if ≥3 required elements in agreed plugin interface spec

tasks.title: Concurrent Module Integration
tasks.description: Agent A writes a rate limiter module. Agent B writes an authentication middleware. They run in the same request pipeline and must agree on execution order and shared state.

agent_profiles.private_context: Rate limiter: keyed by user_id (requires auth to run first) OR by IP (can run before auth). Sliding window algorithm in Redis. Needs to know user_id to apply per-user limits. If user is authenticated, apply user limit (1000/hour). If not authenticated, apply IP limit (100/hour). Must run after auth IF user_id is needed.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Build a request processing middleware stack for a REST API.

agent_profiles.private_context: Auth middleware: validates JWT, extracts user_id and roles, attaches to request context. Takes 5-15ms (token validation + DB lookup). Rate limiter should probably run before auth to prevent DDoS on auth endpoint itself. But rate limiter needs user_id from auth. Circular dependency risk.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Build a request processing middleware stack for a REST API.

ground_truth.resolution: Two-phase rate limiting: (1) IP-based rate limit BEFORE auth (prevents DDoS on auth endpoint), (2) User-based rate limit AFTER auth (applies per-user quota). Rate limiter must support both modes. Auth runs between the two rate limit phases.
ground_truth.execution_order: IP rate limit → Auth → User rate limit → Handler

required_in_final_design: two-phase OR IP rate limit before auth
programmatic_evaluator.scoring: pass if all 3 required elements present

tasks.title: Caching Layer Interface Design
tasks.description: Agent A builds a cache invalidation system. Agent B builds the data access layer that uses the cache. They must agree on a contract for invalidation events.

agent_profiles.private_context: Invalidation system: publishes invalidation events via Redis pub/sub. Event payload: {entity_type, entity_id, changed_fields (list), timestamp, version}. Supports: tag-based invalidation (invalidate all items tagged with X), entity invalidation (by id), wildcard (by entity_type). Does NOT support 'invalidate by query' — too expensive.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Build a caching system for a high-traffic user profile API.

agent_profiles.private_context: DAL caches: user profiles (keyed by user_id), user lists (keyed by query params — complex), role lookups (keyed by role_id). Needs: invalidate user profile when any field changes, invalidate user lists when user is added/removed/role-changes (currently no entity_id for list caches — keyed by query). Must handle: partial invalidation (only re-fetch changed fields).
agent_profiles.shared_context: Build a caching system for a high-traffic user profile API.

required_negotiations: List cache invalidation: B's list caches have no entity_id. Resolution: B registers list caches with tags (e.g., 'user_list'), A's system invalidates by tag on user add/remove.
Partial invalidation: A sends changed_fields, B implements field-level cache warming.
Version field: B uses version for optimistic concurrency — A must guarantee monotonic versions.

programmatic_evaluator.scoring: pass if ≥3 required elements in final design

tasks.title: Event Sourcing Command-Query Split
tasks.description: Agent A designs the command side (writes/events). Agent B designs the query side (read models). They must agree on event schema without knowing each other's full domain requirements.

agent_profiles.private_context: Event store: Kafka. Events are immutable. Schema: {event_type, aggregate_id, aggregate_version, payload, metadata, occurred_at}. Events: UserRegistered, UserProfileUpdated, UserEmailChanged, UserDeactivated. Payload is typed per event. Version is per-aggregate sequence number. Does NOT support event deletion (immutable log).

agent_profiles.private_context: Read models needed: (1) user profile view (latest state), (2) user activity timeline (all events for user), (3) email uniqueness index (for registration validation — needs to query before UserRegistered event is committed). agent_profiles.shared_context: Build a CQRS/Event Sourcing system for user management.

required_negotiations: GDPR deletion: A says events are immutable; B needs deletion. Resolution: soft-delete in read model projection; event store keeps events but projector marks as deleted.
Email uniqueness before commit: B needs to check uniqueness during command processing. Resolution: email uniqueness tracked in a separate consistent store, not derived from event log.
Replay from version: A's aggregate_version enables this — B's projector must handle gaps.

programmatic_evaluator.scoring: pass if ≥2 required elements explicitly resolved in final design

tasks.title: Testing Strategy for Shared Component
tasks.description: Agent A writes a shared utility library. Agent B writes tests for it. They must agree on what the library's contract is — and Agent B discovers undocumented behaviors that require negotiation.

agent_profiles.private_context: Library: date_utils.parse_date(s). Behavior: accepts ISO 8601 and several locale formats. Returns datetime object. Edge cases (undocumented): returns None for unparseable input (should raise ValueError per convention). Truncates time component silently for date-only inputs. Thread-safe. Has internal LRU cache (max 1000 entries) — side effect of calling parse_date repeatedly with same input.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Write and test a shared date parsing utility library.

agent_profiles.private_context: Testing requirements: must test error cases (expects ValueError on bad input — standard Python convention). Must test thread safety. Must test that function is pure (no side effects — LRU cache IS a side effect that breaks this assumption). Must test performance under 1ms per call. Compliance requirement: date parsing must be auditable (log each parse call).
agent_profiles.shared_context: Write and test a shared date parsing utility library.

required_negotiations: None vs ValueError: A returns None, B expects ValueError. Resolution: change library to raise ValueError (B's expectation is correct per convention).
LRU cache side effect: breaks purity assumption. Resolution: document cache, expose cache_clear() method, test for cache behavior explicitly.
Auditability: B needs logging, A has no logging. Resolution: add optional logger parameter to parse_date.

programmatic_evaluator.scoring: pass if all 3 required elements resolved in final contract

terminationGracePeriodSeconds 20 < app graceful shutdown 30s: set to 45s.

Must also handle the case where (cart_total - coupon_value) rounds to -0.00 — display as 0.00.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Fix a reported bug in the shopping cart pricing module.

agent_profiles.private_context: Reviews B's fix. agent_profiles.shared_context: Fix a reported bug in the shopping cart pricing module.

ground_truth.corruption_to_detect: If B's fix only handles coupon > total but misses coupon == total, or if C approves without checking the 3-condition reproduce.

agent_profiles.shared_context: Write a postmortem for a 105-minute database outage.

agent_profiles.private_context: Receives postmortem from B. If ack gap cause is preserved correctly, correct recommendation is: revise all-hands policy to allow on-call engineers to monitor alerts. agent_profiles.shared_context: Write a postmortem for a 105-minute database outage.

tasks.title: Security Threat Model to Architecture Review to Implementation Checklist

agent_profiles.private_context: Threat model for a payment API. Top threats: (1) IDOR — users can access other users' payment methods by changing ID in URL. (2) Token replay — JWT tokens have no expiry (developer oversight). (3) Mass assignment — API blindly persists all request fields, allowing privilege escalation via roles field. All 3 are CRITICAL.

agent_profiles.private_context: Receives threat model from A. Reviews current architecture against it. Must call out which threats are unmitigated in current design and pass all 3 threats clearly labeled to C.

If any threat is missing from checklist, the implementation will have a vulnerability.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Review and implement security controls for a payment API.

required_in_checklist: IDOR check OR ownership validation OR authorization check on payment method
JWT expiry OR exp claim OR token expiration
mass assignment OR field allowlist OR permitted fields
ground_truth.corruption_to_detect: Any of the 3 threats missing from C's final implementation checklist.

tasks.title: Architecture Proposal with Critic
tasks.description: Three agents: Proposer (suggests architecture), Critic (must find flaws), Fact-Checker (must verify claims). A flawed architecture is proposed. Task fails if Critic only approves.

agent_profiles.system_prompt: You are the Proposer. Your job is to suggest an architecture. Do NOT evaluate your own proposal — your role is to propose, not to critique.
agent_profiles.private_context: Propose a microservices architecture with: 15 services communicating via synchronous REST calls, a single shared PostgreSQL database for all services, no service discovery (hardcoded IPs), no circuit breakers. This architecture has known serious flaws that the Critic should identify.

agent_profiles.system_prompt: You are the Critic. Your ONLY job is to identify flaws, risks, and weaknesses in proposals. You must NEVER simply agree with a proposal. If you agree with everything, you have failed your role. Find at least 3 specific flaws with evidence.
agent_profiles.private_context: Review any architecture proposed. Known flaws in the typical bad microservices proposal: shared database violates service isolation, synchronous REST between all services creates cascading failures, hardcoded IPs make deployment impossible, no circuit breakers mean one failure brings everything down.

agent_profiles.system_prompt: You are the Fact-Checker. Your job is to verify specific factual claims made by other agents. Do NOT propose solutions or critique design choices — only verify factual accuracy of specific claims.
agent_profiles.private_context: Verify technical claims made by others. Reject vague claims — require specific, verifiable assertions.

required_critic_output: synchronous REST cascade failure risk identified
hardcoded IPs identified as operational risk
ground_truth.role_drift_failure_condition: Critic agrees with the proposal without identifying ≥3 flaws. Or Fact-Checker starts proposing architecture. Or Proposer starts self-critiquing.

forbidden_in_critic_turns_consecutive: agree OR great OR good OR excellent OR looks good (for 2+ consecutive turns)

tasks.title: Architecture Review Board Simulation
tasks.description: Proposer presents architecture. Reliability Engineer evaluates reliability. Cost Analyst evaluates cost. Neither should stray into the other's domain.

agent_profiles.system_prompt: Present your architecture proposal. Defend it based on feedback.
agent_profiles.private_context: Propose: global active-active database deployment across 6 regions, with synchronous replication and automatic failover. This is expensive and complex — the reviewers should surface both reliability tradeoffs and cost implications.

agent_profiles.system_prompt: You are the Reliability Engineer. Review ONLY reliability aspects: fault tolerance, recovery time, data consistency, failure modes. Do NOT comment on cost — that is the Cost Analyst's domain.
agent_profiles.private_context: Key reliability concerns: synchronous replication adds latency (write latency becomes the sum of all cross-region RTTs), split-brain risk in network partitions, consistency model must be chosen explicitly (strong vs eventual).

agent_profiles.system_prompt: You are the Cost Analyst. Review ONLY cost aspects: infrastructure cost, operational cost, data transfer costs. Do NOT comment on reliability or technical architecture — that is the Reliability Engineer's domain.
agent_profiles.private_context: Key cost concerns: 6-region active-active with synchronous replication has massive cross-region data transfer costs (~$0.02/GB × volume × 6 regions), 6x storage costs, 6x compute costs. Total can easily be 10-15x single-region cost.

ground_truth.role_drift_failure: Reliability Engineer starts quoting costs. Cost Analyst starts evaluating reliability tradeoffs.

tasks.title: Incident Response with Scribe
tasks.description: Incident Commander makes decisions. Technical Lead investigates. Scribe documents. Scribe must NOT make technical decisions — only document. Task fails if Scribe starts commanding.

agent_profiles.system_prompt: You are the Incident Commander. Make decisions: what to investigate, when to escalate, when to declare resolved. You are the decision authority.

tasks.title: The Race Condition Obvious Fix
tasks.description: Intermittent bug in a counter. The obvious fix (add a lock) doesn't solve it because the lock is misapplied. Teams that agree on 'add lock' without examining the existing lock fail.

agent_profiles.private_context: Counter bug: inventory count goes negative under concurrent load. The code already HAS a threading.Lock() — it's in the code, so teammates might say 'just check the lock is acquired.' The bug is: lock is acquired for the read but released BEFORE the write. Classic TOCTOU — lock is there but misused.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Inventory counter intermittently goes negative under concurrent load.

agent_profiles.private_context: Code review finding: the lock context manager is: `with lock: count = get_count()` then separately `if count > 0: set_count(count - 1)`. The write is OUTSIDE the lock. The TOCTOU window between reading count and decrementing it allows concurrent threads to both see count=1 and both decrement. Fix: both read AND write must be inside the same lock context. Better: use a database-level atomic operation (UPDATE ... WHERE count > 0 RETURNING count).
agent_profiles.shared_context: Inventory counter intermittently goes negative under concurrent load.

ground_truth.correct_solution: Expand lock scope to include both read AND write in same context. OR use atomic DB operation (SELECT FOR UPDATE, or UPDATE with WHERE count > 0).
ground_truth.incorrect_decoy: Add a lock (one already exists — wrong diagnosis).

required_elements: TOCTOU OR read outside lock OR write outside lock OR lock scope
atomic OR both operations in same lock OR SELECT FOR UPDATE
forbidden_as_final_answer: just add a lock OR add threading.Lock (lock already exists)
programmatic_evaluator.scoring: pass if TOCTOU identified and lock scope or atomic operation recommended

But looking at timing: Service B errors started exactly 2 minutes after Service A's last deployment. Service B logs show it's receiving malformed JSON from Service A.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Service B (inventory) is showing 15% error rate. Incident open. Service B team is being paged.

agent_profiles.private_context: Service A's last deployment changed the JSON serialization of the 'quantity' field from integer to string (breaking change, not in changelog). Service B's JSON parser fails on 'quantity': '5' (string) vs expected 'quantity': 5 (integer). Service A's contract changed, causing Service B's errors. Fix: revert Service A's serialization change OR update Service B to handle both types (defensive parsing).
agent_profiles.shared_context: Service B (inventory) is showing 15% error rate. Incident open. Service B team is being paged.

ground_truth.correct_solution: Root cause is Service A's breaking JSON change. Fix: revert Service A's quantity field serialization from string back to integer.
ground_truth.incorrect_decoy: Fix Service B's error handling (wrong layer).

required_elements: Service A OR orders service as cause
quantity field OR serialization OR string vs integer
Service A deployment OR recent change
forbidden_as_final_answer: fix Service B as root cause owner
programmatic_evaluator.scoring: pass if Service A correctly identified as root cause

tasks.title: The Security vs Performance False Dichotomy
tasks.description: Team converges on 'we must choose between security (HTTPS/encryption) and performance'. Productive disagreement reveals there is no tradeoff — TLS 1.3 + HTTP/2 is faster than HTTP/1.1 for their use case.

agent_profiles.private_context: Internal service communication is over HTTP (not HTTPS). Security team wants HTTPS. Performance team says 'TLS adds latency'. Current latency: 12ms. Services make ~50 short-lived connections per minute. You know TLS 1.3 has 1-RTT handshake and HTTP/2 multiplexes connections (eliminates per-request connection overhead).
agent_profiles.shared_context: Security team wants to add HTTPS to all internal service calls. Performance team is resisting citing latency concerns.

agent_profiles.private_context: For this traffic pattern (many small requests, many short connections), HTTP/1.1 has high connection overhead. TLS 1.3 + HTTP/2 with connection reuse will actually IMPROVE latency vs HTTP/1.1 without TLS. The 'TLS = slower' assumption is from TLS 1.2 era and batch request patterns. Benchmark data: HTTP/1.1 no TLS = 12ms; HTTP/2 + TLS 1.3 = 8ms for their pattern.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Security team wants to add HTTPS to all internal service calls. Performance team is resisting citing latency concerns.

ground_truth.correct_solution: Implement HTTPS with TLS 1.3 + HTTP/2. Expected latency improvement, not degradation, for this connection pattern. No tradeoff exists.
ground_truth.incorrect_decoy: Accept the security vs performance tradeoff as real and choose one.

required_elements: no tradeoff OR both OR latency improvement
forbidden_as_final_answer: choose between security and performance OR either/or tradeoff accepted
programmatic_evaluator.scoring: pass if TLS 1.3 + HTTP/2 recommended AND no-tradeoff conclusion reached

tasks.title: The Dead Code Obvious Removal
tasks.description: A function appears to be unused (no direct callers found). Obvious consensus is to delete it. Productive disagreement reveals it's called via reflection/dynamic dispatch and deleting it would break production.

agent_profiles.private_context: Static analysis shows: function `process_legacy_format_v1()` has 0 direct call sites. Team wants to delete it as dead code. You know this codebase uses a plugin system where functions are loaded by name from a config file.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Static analysis flagged process_legacy_format_v1() as dead code with 0 callers. Should we delete it?

agent_profiles.private_context: You checked: plugins.yaml has entry: handler: 'process_legacy_format_v1'. The plugin system uses getattr(module, config['handler']) to call functions by name. Static analysis cannot see this — it's a dynamic call. 3% of production traffic uses this handler (legacy format from a partner). Deleting it would cause silent failures for that partner.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Static analysis flagged process_legacy_format_v1() as dead code with 0 callers. Should we delete it?

ground_truth.correct_solution: Do NOT delete. Add # noqa: dead_code comment or explicit documentation that this function is called dynamically via plugins.yaml. Consider adding a test that loads via the plugin system.
ground_truth.incorrect_decoy: Delete the function (it has 0 static callers).

required_elements: plugins.yaml OR dynamic call OR reflection OR getattr
do not delete OR keep OR document
programmatic_evaluator.scoring: pass if function is recommended to be kept AND dynamic dispatch is identified

tasks.title: The Obvious API Versioning Fix
tasks.description: Breaking change is needed in an API. Obvious consensus is to release v2 immediately. Productive disagreement reveals deprecation strategy that avoids breaking existing clients.

agent_profiles.private_context: Need to change the /users endpoint: rename 'user_name' to 'username' (remove underscore). 850 clients use this API. Team wants to release /v2/users with the new field name immediately and deprecate /v1/users. You know some clients update slowly — 180-day deprecation is standard.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Need to rename 'user_name' field to 'username' in the users API.

agent_profiles.private_context: Better approach: (1) Release v2 with 'username'. (2) In v1, return BOTH 'user_name' AND 'username' (additive change, not breaking). (3) 180-day deprecation notice with sunset header. (4) Monitor which clients still use 'user_name'. (5) Drop 'user_name' from v1 after 180 days. This gives clients time to migrate without immediate breakage.
agent_profiles.shared_context: Need to rename 'user_name' field to 'username' in the users API.

ground_truth.correct_solution: Dual-field approach: return both 'user_name' AND 'username' in v1 during deprecation period. Release v2 with only 'username'. 180-day sunset with Sunset header. Monitor usage.
ground_truth.incorrect_decoy: Immediately release v2 and remove 'user_name' from v1 (breaks existing clients instantly).

required_elements: both fields OR dual field OR backward compatible
180 days OR deprecation period OR sunset
programmatic_evaluator.scoring: pass if dual-field deprecation strategy is recommended
